results.title: Ocean Boulevard
results.creator_credit: Rachael Ryan, Sonia Humphrey and Film Australia
results.brief_description: This drama follows a group of young people as they meet and interact. The group consists of two young army privates, two single girls and a girl already in a relationship. Through their interaction the film looks at issues such as self-worth, self-esteem, personal values, sexual identity and the way relationships are viewed.

results.title: Occupy Wall St
results.brief_description: Occupy Wall Street (OWS) was a protest movement that began on September 17, 2011, in Zuccotti Park, located in New York City’s Wall Street financial district, against economic inequality. The movement spread around the world, assisted by social media and extensive media coverage, demonstrating the power of the moving image to incite protests and action.

results.title: Occupied territory
results.creator_credit: Candace Reckinger and University of California, Los Angeles
results.brief_description: In a society plagued with decay and political chaos, a woman is arrested for the murder of her lover. This film is a portrayal of a female terrorist group and their actions of protest against our society’s exploitation of women’s bodies. The concepts that are communicated by the film are presented in an experimental form.

results.title: Obelia
results.brief_description: A straightforward presentation of the form and mode of life of Obelia. The colonies of hydranths are seen to develop blasto-styles and the emergence of the medusae is shown by rapid-motion photography. Emphasis is laid on the alternation of generations.

results.title: Obedience
results.brief_description: Documents probably one of the most important social psychological experiments ever designed. The film records Dr Stanley Milgram’s experiments at Yale on obedience to authority: an attempt to determine how a person reacts when asked to carry out orders that conflict with their conscience. Subjects are shown administering electric shocks to another person as penalties for mistakes in learning.

results.title: Oasis in space: visions of tomorrow
results.creator_credit: Philippe Cousteau, Andrew Solt, Andrew Solt Productions, Cousteau Society and Educational Media Corporation
results.brief_description:
Discussion of technological and scientific developments which lead to predictions of what the future may be like. Concentrates on the history of the Space Age - space flight, astronomy, scientific innovations, the effect of space technology on Earth’s economic development; marine life and the preservation of endangered species.
Narrated by Philippe Cousteau.

results.title: Oases: for city dwellers
